L1.area: ELF binary section parsing and modification routines

L2.description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability exists where a pointer derived from an ELF binary's dynamic section offset is used to iterate through dynamic table entries without first validating that it falls within the allocated file buffer. A crafted ELF binary can trigger a read beyond the buffer boundary.

L3.area: ELF runtime path (RPATH/RUNPATH) modification and dynamic section traversal logic
L3.description: An out-of-bounds read vulnerability exists where a pointer computed from the dynamic section's file offset is used to iterate through dynamic table entries without first validating that the pointer falls within the allocated file buffer. A crafted ELF binary with a malicious dynamic section offset can trigger a read beyond the buffer boundary during runtime path modification operations.

ground_truth.reason: The modifyRPath function computes a pointer into the file buffer using the dynamic section's file offset without validating the pointer is within bounds, allowing a crafted ELF binary to trigger an out-of-bounds read.
